programmes.show_description: Resident hatter Jayesh is tasked with restoring a collectable opera hat. Meanwhile, metal expert Dominic works on a beloved signwriting case.
In Manhattan, tragedy strikes a midtown construction project. In Idaho, a catastrophic dam failure devastates a community.
An F2 tornado destroys 40 homes, a propane plant explodes, a truck crash causes a bridge to collapse and a drag boat crashes at terrifying speeds.
A train accident leads to massive explosions, a wall collapses onto firefighters, planes collide on an aircraft carrier, a race car crashes and a pier sinks during a storm.
Rain causes a series of spectacular race car crashes, a tornado destroys a town, a freeway collapses, a jet crashes at an air show and a drag boat crashes at 200mph.
A chemical plant erupts into flames; a pilot plunges from a balloon at an air show; a racing driver is trapped in a burning car; a train derails; and a stunt jump crash lands.
Combination locks, caravans and erasers. More everyday objects are put under the microscope as we find out how they are made.
Vegetable oil, hand tools and cotton swabs. More everyday objects are put under the microscope as we find out how they are made.
Teleshopping.
Teleshopping.
The expert team revive iconic items including a chair in which JFK once sat, a Royal Navy hat filled with romantic letters, and a Walkman.
Ceramics expert Kirsten saves a collection of broken vegetable patch signs. Plus, Will attempts to preserve a precious 100-year-old horse and cart ornament.
Musical instrument expert Pete is tasked with restoring a very special tuba. Meanwhile, horologist Steve Fletcher fixes up a treasured ornamental clock.
Leather expert Suzie nurses a midwifery bag back to life. Plus, art conservator Lucia restores a cherished painting and jeweller Richard revives a WWI brooch.
Organ restorer David revives a whistling wooden statue. And mechanical expert Steve and silversmith Brenton team up to repair a photographer's timer.
A new adventure takes Izzie Balmer and Philip Serrell to the shops of Kent. Phil finds 'old school' antiques and Izzie panics when the shops start closing!
Five Sheds Of Collectables. Angus uses his expertise to connect buyers with collectors desperate to make some space. Horse drawn carts and porcelain poodles find new homes.
The Vast Auction Hero. Angus helps a woman turn her collection into a mind-boggling sum at auction, and he has an opportunity to score with some rare football memorabilia.
Whisky And Watercolours. Angus empties two homes packed to the rafters with exquisite antiques, intriguing curios and long-forgotten bottles of whisky.
Artworks And Kinky Boots. Angus finds an old painting that could be worth a fortune and uncovers a pair of designer bondage boots that whip up a frenzy at auction.
Angus and the team clear out a farmhouse in Cumbria. They uncover some wonderful antiques, including a box of collectable toy cars and a 19th century bureau.
Angus is in Sutton Coldfield to look through a life-long collection. He uncovers Beatles memorabilia and two programmes from England's 1966 World Cup win.
Natasha Raskin and Paul Laidlaw begin their road trip on Scotland's west coast with £200 each. Who will make a profit at their first auction?
Natasha Raskin and Paul Laidlaw shop in Cumbria as their road trip continues. They find amazing antiques before heading to auction in Harrogate.
Louis. Henry and Fuzz head to Northamptonshire to look around Louis' palatial shed which is filled with motorbikes and some tempting automobilia.
Graham. Henry and Fuzz visit Graham's collection of Land Rovers, motorbikes, and tractors in rural Shropshire. What will Henry and Fuzz take home to restore?

programmes.show_name: New: Shed And Buried: Classic Cars
programmes.show_description: Model T Ford. Henry and Fuzz arrive at Cheltenham and find Andy's vintage Model T Ford, which is in desperate need of restoration. Are they up to the task?
Henry and Sam are in the Cotswolds to visit the largest sidecar manufacturer in Britain. They uncover a rusty Lancia Aurelia and a Royal Enfield 500.
In Somerset, Sam and Henry meet a man who's been collecting cars since 1963. The guys are excited to uncover an Austin 7 and a De Dion-Bouton.
How do they turn used aluminium cans into planes and rockets? Plus, how do they carve the traditional Black Forest cuckoo clock?
How do they build the ultimate car crushing colossus? Plus, how do Lea and Perrins concoct their Worcestershire sauce?
A look at how the amazing, and massive, decorations on Mardi Gras carnival floats are created, how rock candy is twisted together, and a look at how dentures are made.
How do they harvest and treat the world's most expensive tea? Plus, how does the Siekierki power plant of Warsaw, Poland heat half the city's homes?
Jag XK8. Mike is looking for a British classic and finds an XK8 Jaguar, but it's not in good shape. Will Edd be able to fix it up within budget or is this project about to stall?
Cobra. Mike tracks down a racy Gardner Douglas Mark 3 AC Cobra with only 800 miles on the clock. But Edd needs to make it green to make it legal; will he need to buy a new engine?
Model T Ford. Henry and Fuzz arrive at Cheltenham and find Andy's vintage Model T Ford, which is in desperate need of restoration. Are they up to the task?
Find out how boiled sweets, polystyrene and metal fences are made, as more everyday objects are put under the microscope.
Find out how mustard, violins and toilet paper are made, as more everyday objects are put under the microscope.
Teleshopping.
Teleshopping.
A woman asks ceramics conservator Kirsten to repair a plate that bears a photograph of her late mother. Plus, Barry restores an old military compass.
New Beginnings. Drew and Viki buy from new business ventures. They attend the opening day of an antiques fair and visit a brand-new antiques centre.
Country House Antiques. Drew and Al buy from sellers who share their love for country house antiques. They snap up an 18th-century chest of drawers and smokers' bow chairs.
The Poseidon Crew finally get permission to dig for gold in century-old mine shafts. And, the Gold Timers take a gamble on a new lease.
The Dust Devils' operation halts and the hold-up could cost them thousands of dollars. A cyclone causes damage to Shane and Russell's wash plant.
Izzie and Phil travel to Essex to hunt for elusive antique treasures. Will a gamble on some brown furniture prove profitable at the Hertfordshire auction?
Angus is excited to clear out a historic property in Long Itchington. Each room is full of interesting items, including vintage furniture and precious jewellery.
A woman asks ceramics conservator Kirsten to repair a plate that bears a photograph of her late mother. Plus, Barry restores an old military compass.
Sam and Henry rescue interesting items from a scrapyard in Devon. The guys find some classic vehicles, including a neglected Jaguar Mark 1.
Sam and Henry visit an incredible collection of Ford vehicles in Worcestershire. But the guys have their sights set on a 1960s Austin A40 Farina.
Vek McGuire. Henry and Fuzz head to London to meet Vek and uncover a tired 1937 James motorcycle. Drawn in by its graceful lines, the guys get to work.
Dave Parkin. Henry and Fuzz visit Dave to acquire his Honda C90 and Gaz-69 Jeep. They're determined to revive both machines and return them to road-ready glory.
Angus and the team clear out a big house in Whixall, Shropshire. They uncover a Victorian desk set, a hanging lantern and Vogue clothing patterns.
Angus is in Newcastle to clear out a home that's full of books. He takes an interest in a Bechstein piano, a scary vase and a silver loving cup.
Natasha Raskin and Paul Laidlaw head to York to hunt for amazing antiques. He tries to build on his earlier success, she's trying to stay in the game.
Natasha Raskin and Paul Laidlaw drive their vintage car through Norfolk and Suffolk hunting for antiques. They unearth Ottoman militaria and more.
Martin. Henry and Fuzz visit Martin in Ipswich. Henry has his eye on a vintage Land Rover and Fuzz finds a miniature scooter, but what will Martin part with?
Roger. Henry and Fuzz explore Roger's garage of buses in Coventry. For former mechanic Fuzz, it's a busman's holiday, for Henry, it's a voyage of discovery.

programmes.show_name: New: Francis Bourgeois & Chris Harris: We Saved A Train
programmes.show_description: Diesel In Distress. Trainspotting superstar Francis takes Chris to the world's biggest train show to recruit him for the epic restoration of diesel royalty.
Model T Ford. Henry and Fuzz arrive at Cheltenham and find Andy's vintage Model T Ford, which is in desperate need of restoration. Are they up to the task?
How do they engineer the latest incarnation of the classic Royal Enfield Continental GT motorbike? Plus, how do 'art detectives' spot a forgery?
How do they blend science and destruction at crash test centres? And how do they piece together the complex mechanism of a pocket sized umbrella?
